kept.text:
🇺🇸 Alphabet (Google) $GOOG 

Cette semaine, je passe en revue les 7 fantastiques. On commence avec Alphabet 👇

Un business exceptionnel, mais pas sans questions.

Le groupe combine Search, YouTube, Android, Cloud et IA, avec une rentabilité très élevée et un bilan solide. 

Probablement l’une qui a le mix le plus équilibré.

Les derniers résultats publiés semaine dernière ont été extrêmement bons et salués par le marché, avec un chiffre d’affaires de 109,9 milliards de dollars, en hausse de 22% sur un an, et un bénéfice net de 62,6 milliards de dollars, en hausse de 81%. 

Finalement, la société continue de démontrer que l’impact de l’IA sur la recherche traditionnelle reste marginal.

Fiche complète ci-dessous 👇

quoted_tweet.text:
5 Stocks with Strong Moats

$GOOG - Alphabet

Alphabet is executing a differentiated, vertically integrated AI full-stack strategy, which includes:
Infrastructure, Models, Platforms, Applications, and Agents. This approach is now visibly accelerating revenue, margins, and backlog while sustaining its core advertising strength.

Alphabet is executing a differentiated vertically integrated AI full-stack strategy, which include:
Infrastructure → models → platforms → applications → agents, which is now visibly accelerating revenue, margins, and backlog while sustaining core advertising strength.

They continue to dominate global digital advertising through Google Search and YouTube, supported by powerful network effects and unmatched data scale. 

Search volume has been further boosted by the adoption of Google Lens, which has increased usage by approximately 70%.

YouTube remains a key growth engine, expanding into live sports, connected TV, and digital advertising. Annual revenue has surpassed $60 billion, and the platform has maintained its position as the #1 streaming platform in the U.S. for nearly three consecutive years.

The company’s subscription ecosystem is also accelerating, with 350 million paid subscriptions, driven by offerings such as YouTube Premium, Google One, and the Gemini AI platform. Gemini Enterprise paid MAUs grew +40% QoQ, while generative AI cloud revenue surged +800% YoY, reinforcing Alphabet’s full-stack leadership in AI.

AI integration across products continues to drive engagement. AI-powered tools supported over 1 million channels daily, while features like Ask (used by ~20 million users) enhance user interaction. Also, AI Overviews now reach more than 2 billion monthly users across 200+ countries, increasing query volume by around 10% in supported categories.

Google Cloud is another major growth driver, with backlog nearly doubling QoQ to $460 billion and expected to grow 50% over the next 24 months, fueled by enterprise and public-sector AI adoption.

Despite aggressive AI investment, Alphabet maintains exceptional cash generation, allowing it to fund innovation while preserving strong profitability, making it one of the few companies capable of combining scale, growth, and financial resilience.

kept.text:
We are pleased to have accurately predicted GCP's 63% YoY growth ahead of $GOOG 's earnings — this marks the third time in a row.

We turned bullish on GOOG starting from 2Q25. At that time, it was because we carefully studied AI Overview and found that AI Overview is incremental revenue for GOOG Ads. GOOG used many clever methods. For example, for high-value queries, GOOG would still insert AI Overview even after already placing three Page Ranking Ads. And for queries that originally had no Page Ranking Ads, GOOG was more aggressive in adding AI Overview. So GOOG has actually been comparing each query to decide what kind of ads to add. They need to make sure AI Overview is incremental revenue.

In 3Q25, we wrote a report on GCP titled "The Path to $100Bn." We were the first to call for GCP to reach 45% growth in 2026, which was the most bullish view on the Street at the time. Honestly, I was also surprised when I built that model. But we didn't expect GOOG to achieve it as early as 4Q25.

In our 1Q26 Preview, we wrote that GOOG would achieve 63% growth in 1Q26 and exceed 70% in the following three quarters. This is mainly driven by:

TPU cloud
GPU cloud
TPU direct sales
Gemini ARR — 70% of Gemini ARR will be booked into GCP, and it grew 50% QoQ.

quoted_tweet.text:
Review| $GOOG / $MSFT / $AMZN : CapEx Raised — 2026 Finally Becomes the Year of Cloud Acceleration

Q: Is 2026 the most pronounced year of CSP acceleration?

A: Unequivocally yes. GPU prices began rising in 1Q26, with CPU prices following from 2Q26. The incremental growth this year is also coming from new vectors that weren’t material before — namely coding APIs (AWS-Bedrock, GCP-Vertex, Azure-AI Foundry) and ASICs (AWS-Trainium, GCP-TPU).

Q: Will CapEx continue to be revised higher?

A: Yes. As we discussed in our Weekly Meeting, the LTAs (long-term agreements) signed between CSPs and the memory makers took effect from March–April. This means all forward CapEx must be marked to the LTA floor price — implying a structural step-up regardless of demand. Critically, LTAs lock in floors but do not cap upside, so any further spot price appreciation will likely drive additional CapEx revisions.

Q: Why did GCP and Azure come in fully in line with our expectations, while AWS missed?
......

Detailed Report
https://t.co/MnM21MYYML
